abundance.title: How to write for Works in Progress
abundance.snippet: We are interested in a wide range of article types. Some of our best articles have been journalism about breakthroughs in science and technology, like Stephan Guyenet's early piece on the effectiveness of GLP-1 agonists like semaglutide, and Hiawatha Bray's on the many uses for silk. One of our pieces is a 'how to' - Nan Ransohoff's guide to creating an advance market commitment ...

abundance.title: German housing crisis: government plans construction boost
abundance.snippet: Germanyhas a desperate shortage of affordablehousing. The government now plans to take a "crowbar" toconstructionlaw to help get more homes built at "turbo" speed.

abundance.title: Seven YIMBY Victories in 2025 You Should Know About
abundance.snippet: We've tracked over 196 state and local policy victories thatYIMBYAction chapters supported in2025. And when you realize that our tracking isn't perfect, and thatYIMBYAction is far from the onlyYIMBYorganization… that means that the pro-housingmovement is accelerating at a truly astounding rate.

articles.title: What can the world’s most walkable cities teach other places?
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: Some cities, like Copenhagen, Paris, and Tokyo, are designed to be very walkable, with everything residents need close by. These "15-minute cities" allow people to access shops, schools, and parks easily on foot. A recent study highlights how other urban areas can learn from these examples to improve their walkability.
articles.reason: Ciudades caminables como modelo urbano óptimo

articles.title: America’s huge mortgage market is slowly dying
articles.author: Finance & economics
articles.summary: America’s mortgage market, once the heart of the American dream, is shrinking. Government support for home loans is fading. This decline may worsen the housing crisis.
articles.reason: Crisis estructural del sueño americano inmobiliario

articles.title: PG&E Project Aims to Ease AI Computing Need and Housing Shortage
articles.author: Mark Chediak
articles.summary: PG&E is partnering with Westbank to build a zero-carbon data center and housing project in downtown San Jose. The plan includes three data centers and up to 4,000 new homes. Excess heat from the data centers will be used to heat nearby buildings.

articles.title: It’s Electricity Realism, Not Climate Denialism
articles.author: Javier Blas
articles.summary: The global energy system is undergoing a major change due to the electrification of everything. However, this transition faces challenges from cultural debates and the need to address risks associated with electric technologies. As electricity demand continues to rise, it’s crucial for advocates of clean energy solutions to confront these issues.

articles.title: A strong month in a fragile market
articles.author: Halina Bennet
articles.summary: March data shows momentum, but a build-to-rent crackdown and tighter capital markets threaten to stall future supply.
articles.reason: Análisis build-to-rent y oferta futura vivienda

articles.title: Lo que nos dicen las revisiones del INE: las empresas han ganado más y los salarios, menos
articles.author: Javier Jorrín
articles.summary: La revisión de las series muestra un comportamiento más dinámico de los beneficios y más pobre de los salarios. Se corrigen discrepancias con otras fuentes estadísticas
articles.reason: Distribución ingresos trabajo vs capital datos

articles.title: Raising employment among women and older workers: A policy lever for ageing economies
articles.author: VoxEU | CEPR
articles.summary: Aging populations and low birth rates are causing fewer people to work, which hurts the economy. Increasing jobs for women and older workers can help fix this problem. Policies like better childcare, flexible work, and later retirement can boost employment and support growth.
articles.reason: Productividad demográfica: mujeres y mayores trabajando

articles.title: China’s Coal Industry Has a Big, Dirty Secret
articles.author: Javier Blas
articles.summary: China's coal industry has a massive and often overlooked sector that converts coal into chemical products and liquid fuels, consuming about 380 million metric tons of coal. This industry is expanding rapidly, despite China’s efforts to transition to green energy, and poses significant challenges for global climate goals. The reliance on coal for energy security and economic stability means that coal demand in China is likely to remain high for the foreseeable future.

articles.title: How politics shapes the world’s time zones
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: Politics heavily influences time zones around the world, often leading to significant discrepancies between neighboring regions. Countries may adjust their time zones for national identity, economic reasons, or political statements. Examples include China's unified "Beijing time" and Spain's continued use of Central European Time due to historical decisions.
articles.reason: Geopolítica oculta en husos horarios globales

articles.title: La brecha ideológica de los jóvenes se agranda: ellos, cada vez más de derechas y ellas, más de izquierdas
articles.author: Javier Jorrín
articles.summary: Por primera vez en la época reciente surge una diferenciación ideológica grande dentro de una generación. Los hombres jóvenes viven una realidad hostil que genera resentimiento
articles.reason: Polarización generacional por género en datos

articles.title: Our Carrie Bradshaw index shows Australia’s housing is in crisis
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: Australia is facing a housing crisis as many struggle to afford homes. House prices have doubled compared to incomes over the last 25 years. The rental market is overwhelmed with demand and has a lack of supply.
articles.reason: Crisis de vivienda australiana con datos

articles.title: Bruselas avisa: España es el país que más recaudación tendrá que destinar a las pensiones
articles.author: Javier Jorrín
articles.summary: La generosidad del sistema español y el rápido envejecimiento que va a vivir el país llevará a destinar el 46% de los ingresos fiscales (incluyendo cotizaciones) a pagar las pensiones
articles.reason: Insostenibilidad fiscal del sistema pensiones español

articles.title: AI Boom Is Driving a Surprise Resurgence of US Gas-Fired Power
articles.author: Josh Saul
articles.summary: US energy companies are rapidly building new natural gas power plants to meet rising demand. This demand is driven by AI data centers, manufacturing, and electric vehicles. Experts are surprised by the continued growth of gas as a major energy source in the country.

articles.title: How “bloated” are governments really?
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: Donald Trump and Elon Musk claim that the federal government is too large and wasteful, leading to mass layoffs of public-sector workers. Musk's Department of Government Efficiency has already cut 56,000 jobs, with more layoffs expected. Other countries, like Argentina and Britain, are also reducing government staff to improve efficiency.
articles.reason: Tamaño real del sector público con datos

articles.title: Así murió la democracia alemana de Weimar (¿podría morir igual la nuestra?)
articles.author: Ramón González Férriz
articles.summary: Un nuevo y brillante libro reconstruye los catorce años de la república alemana de entreguerras y cuenta cómo las malas decisiones provocaron su final de la mano de Hitler

articles.title: Can AI be trusted in schools?
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: AI tools like ChatGPT are causing issues in schools by making students overly reliant on them for assignments. In some countries, when these tools were removed, students struggled more than those who didn't use them. Additionally, some teachers are using AI to provide generic feedback, leading to complaints from students.
articles.reason: IA en educación requiere evidencia empírica

articles.title: GE Vernova to Power City-Sized Data Centers With Gas as AI Demand Soars
articles.author: Naureen S Malik
articles.summary: GE Vernova has signed contracts to provide natural gas turbines for large data centers that will use enough energy to power major cities. These turbines are being reserved by big tech companies for their upcoming 5-gigawatt data center projects. The companies plan to have some of these facilities operational by 2028.

articles.title: Who is ahead in the global tech race?
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: A new index from Harvard ranks 25 countries based on their technological strength in areas like AI, semiconductors, and biotechnology. The United States leads the rankings, but other nations are catching up. This competition impacts economic growth, military power, and global influence.
articles.reason: Ranking global de fortaleza tecnológica por países

articles.title: Ahora o nunca: Europa necesita ambición para recuperar el crecimiento económico
articles.author: J. Jorrín
articles.summary: El escenario económico de Europa era malo antes de Trump, y ahora es peor. Pero la UE tiene una oportunidad para reaccionar y realizar las reformas estructurales que funcionan

articles.title: AI Boom Has Generac Looking to Data Centers for Growth
articles.author: Mark Chediak
articles.summary: Generac Holdings Inc. is expanding from home backup generators to focus on data centers. The company is investing $130 million to produce larger generators for AI applications. They have started shipping these new generators to international data center customers.
articles.reason: Boom IA impulsa crecimiento de centros datos

articles.title: Xcel CEO Says AI Power Needs Raise Prospect of New Large Nuclear
articles.author: Josh Saul
articles.summary: Bob Frenzel Photographer: Kent Nishimura/Bloomberg
articles.reason: Nuclear para satisfacer demanda energética de IA

articles.title: Madrid es la CCAA que más peso gana en el PIB nacional, Cataluña y Euskadi, las que pierden
articles.author: Javier Jorrín
articles.summary: El crecimiento económico sigue girando en torno a la capital, que supone casi el 20% del total nacional. Las CCAA despobladas sostienen su PIB per cápita por las pensiones
articles.reason: Concentración económica Madrid vs dispersión nacional

articles.title: Data Centers Will Have to Make Minimum Power Payments in Ohio
articles.author: Mark Chediak
articles.summary: Data centers in Ohio will now be required to pay at least 85% of their energy costs to support grid improvements. This agreement was made by American Electric Power Co. and various stakeholders, including the state regulator and Walmart. The settlement aims to ensure that energy infrastructure can meet growing demands.

articles.title: AI to Prop Up Fossil Fuels and Slow Emissions Decline, BNEF Says
articles.author: Shoko Oda
articles.summary: A new report from BNEF suggests that the growth of AI will hinder global efforts to reduce emissions in the next decade. The rise of AI technology is expected to support fossil fuel usage. This could slow down the decline in carbon emissions.
articles.reason: IA ralentiza descarbonización por demanda energética

articles.title: Blackstone Makes $5.7 Billion Bet on Power With Deal to Buy TXNM
articles.author: Mark Chediak
articles.summary: Blackstone Infrastructure is set to buy TXNM Energy Inc. for $5.7 billion. TXNM, which serves over 800,000 homes in New Mexico and Texas, was previously trying to be acquired by another company but that deal fell through. The acquisition reflects a total value of $11.5 billion, including debt.
articles.reason: Blackstone apuesta por sector eléctrico con AI

articles.title: España es el gran país europeo donde los funcionarios más cobran frente al sector privado
articles.author: Javier Jorrín
articles.summary: La brecha salarial alcanza el 9%, sólo superada por Chipre y Luxemburgo. En España, los gobiernos emplean las retribuciones públicas como herramienta electoral
articles.reason: Distorsiones salariales sector público vs privado

articles.title: US Regulator Rejects Amazon-Talen Nuclear Power Agreement
articles.author: Naureen S Malik
articles.summary: The Federal Energy Regulatory Commission rejected an agreement that would have allowed an Amazon Inc.com data center to directly tap into power from an Pennsylvania nuclear plant.

articles.title: Did international trade really kill American manufacturing?
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: President Trump claims that international trade has hurt American manufacturing and aims to bring jobs back to the U.S. through tariffs. However, data shows that the decline in manufacturing jobs is part of larger economic trends, not just trade policies. In 1950, nearly one in three workers were in manufacturing, but today it's only one in twelve.
articles.reason: Comercio internacional no mató manufactura americana

articles.title: Demand for American degrees has already hit covid-era lows
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: Demand for American degrees has dropped to levels seen during the COVID-19 pandemic. Many successful American companies were started by immigrants who came to study in the U.S. A significant number of these startups were founded by individuals who first arrived as students.
articles.reason: Declive demanda universitaria limita talento migratorio

articles.title: ¿Se acabó apretarse el cinturón? Hogares y empresas se endeudan 15 años después
articles.author: Javier Jorrín
articles.summary: El pasivo del sector privado ha vuelto a crecer tras un largo ajuste. El crecimiento económico, la bajada de los tipos de interés y la revalorización de los activos están borrando las preocupaciones del pasado

articles.title: Lessons From the First Blackout of the Green Era
articles.author: Javier Blas
articles.summary: Spain and Portugal experienced a major blackout, affecting over 50 million people, due to issues with renewable energy generation. The power failure was likely caused by a solar plant underperforming, leading to a significant drop in electricity from other renewable sources. Authorities are now analyzing the incident to improve the integration of renewable energy into the grid for future stability.

articles.title: Which countries would benefit most from an American brain drain?
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: The U.S. is becoming less welcoming to talented immigrants due to recent policy changes. This has led other countries to strategize on attracting American graduates and professionals. An analysis identifies which nations could benefit most from this potential brain drain.
articles.reason: Fuga cerebros americana beneficiaría países competidores

articles.title: Europa cae en la trampa de las tierras raras: China produce, EEUU gestiona y la UE paga
articles.author: Javier Jorrín

articles.title: El INE eleva al 0,8% el crecimiento del PIB del segundo trimestre, el mejor dato en un año
articles.author: Javier Jorrín
articles.summary: Estadística eleva en una décima su primera estimación del PIB. El consumo de los hogares y la inversión explican el crecimiento. En tasa interanual, el crecimiento acelera al 3,1%

articles.title: Self-driving cars will transform urban economies
articles.author: Finance & economics
articles.summary: Self-driving robotaxis are starting to change how people travel in cities. These cars work differently from regular cars and will reshape urban areas. This change will be bigger than anything since cars were first invented.

articles.title: Distintas recetas, mismo final: la Argentina de Milei se asoma al abismo financiero
articles.author: Javier Jorrín
articles.summary: La obsesión del Gobierno por mantener sobrevalorado el peso está provocando el vaciamiento de reservas del banco central. EEUU planea intervenir para frenar la tormenta

articles.title: DeepSeek’s AI Model Just Upended the White-Hot US Power Market
articles.author: Michelle Ma
articles.summary: DeepSeek has developed an AI model that has significantly impacted the US power market. This new technology is changing how energy is analyzed and traded. The shift could lead to major changes in the industry's operations.
articles.reason: DeepSeek revoluciona mercado energético con IA

articles.title: The economics of the second Trump administration
articles.author: VoxEU | CEPR
articles.summary: President Trump's second term has caused economic problems like higher inflation, growing inequality, and rising living costs for many Americans. His policies, including tariffs and attacks on independent institutions, have created uncertainty that hurts jobs and investment. While the stock market has done well, most benefits go to the wealthy, leaving everyday people feeling frustrated and left behind.

articles.title: The mystery of China’s slumping investment
articles.author: Finance & economics
articles.summary: China’s investment in infrastructure, manufacturing and construction has fallen sharply. The drops are so large some analysts doubt the data. Officials do not seem very worried.
articles.reason: Misterio desplome inversión china y credibilidad

articles.title: Donald Trump’s tariffs defy geopolitical logic
articles.author: Graphic detail
articles.summary: President Donald Trump announced new tariffs that contradict geopolitical logic, impacting both allies and adversaries. He plans to impose a 10% duty on China and a 25% duty on Mexico and Canada, although the latter tariffs will be paused for a month. These measures aim to address trade surpluses and drug trafficking issues with these countries.

articles.title: California’s Grid Being Saved by Batteries, PG&E CEO Says
articles.author: Mark Chediak
articles.summary: California's electrical grid has been maintained during extreme heat thanks to the rapid use of batteries, according to PG&E's CEO. These batteries store energy, allowing the utility to provide power when needed. As a result, residents have not been asked to conserve energy this summer.

articles.title: Aranceles para reducir déficits: la receta equivocada de Trump

articles.title: Which goods are most vulnerable to American tariffs on China?

articles.summary: President Trump has imposed an additional 10% tariff on all Chinese goods, escalating the trade war between the U.S. and China. This move will have significant effects on various trade flows, but not all goods will be equally impacted. Charts in the article highlight which products are most vulnerable to these tariffs.

articles.title: One weird trick to solve the affordability crisis
articles.author: Finance & economics
articles.summary: Americans have long protested rising food prices, like eggs and meat, causing social unrest. Past government attempts to control prices led to shortages and more inflation. The article suggests a simple, nominal change could help fix the affordability crisis.

articles.title: Trump Won’t Stop California’s Clean Power Push, Official Says
articles.author: David R Baker
articles.summary: California is confident it will achieve 100% clean energy despite President Trump's climate policies. The state's economy has grown while reducing carbon emissions, making it the fifth largest economy in the world. California's energy regulator believes the state can succeed in its clean energy goals while still finding common ground with the federal government.

articles.title: Ten indicators explain what’s going on with America’s economy

articles.title: Recessions have become ultra-rare. That is storing up trouble
articles.author: Finance & economics
articles.summary: Recessions used to happen very often in the past. Over time, they became less common as economies grew and policies improved. Now, recessions are very rare, but this may cause problems in the future.

articles.title: Narendra Modi plans to free up India’s giant labour force
articles.author: Finance & economics
articles.summary: India's labour laws limit the size of factories and the type of workers they hire. This keeps many firms small and relies heavily on contract workers. Prime Minister Narendra Modi plans to remove these restrictions to boost employment and growth.

articles.title: Five Key Charts to Watch in Global Commodity Markets This Week
articles.author: Emma Sanchez
articles.summary: Oil prices are low, around $60 a barrel, leading to a decline in US drilling rigs due to fears of falling demand. Global emissions are expected to drop, but increased fossil fuel use for AI data centers could slow this progress. Meanwhile, China is controlling exports of rare earth metals, which are crucial for technology and defense, while US corn exports are rising due to increased demand.

articles.title: How disposable diapers conquered the world

articles.summary: Benjamin Spock told mothers in the mid twentieth century to buy six dozen cloth diapers and a covered pail. Within a decade, both were obsolete.

articles.title: US Solar Firm Enphase to Move Battery Manufacturing Outside of China

articles.summary: Enphase Energy Inc., a US solar and battery systems supplier, is in the process of moving its battery cell manufacturing out of China to avoid tariffs imposed by President Donald Trump as part of a wider trade war between the world’s two largest economies.

articles.title: US Imposes New Duties on Solar Imports From Southeast Asia

articles.title: Visa restrictions are bad for Indians—but maybe not for India

The proposed changes aim to dismantle parts of Biden's climate law, which some Republicans criticize as a "green new scam."

articles.title: El Gobierno sube al 2,7% su previsión de crecimiento: "Estamos en un círculo virtuoso"
articles.author: Javier Jorrín

articles.author: Mark Chediak

Concerns are rising, leading to a 20% drop in shares for Edison International, the largest utility in the area.

articles.title: Trump’s first term coincided with a MAGA baby boom. Will his second?

This trend resulted in a shift of 1-2% of American births from blue to red counties. As the 2024 election approaches, it's uncertain if a similar baby boom will occur during Trump's potential second term.

articles.title: Don’t blame AI for your job woes

articles.title: Why are girls still falling behind in maths?

articles.title: Economists get cold feet about high minimum wages
articles.author: Finance & economics

articles.summary: History tells us engineers and entrepreneurs will deliver the US from the current hysteria.

articles.title: Hard-right parties are now Europe’s most popular

articles.title: Is an Upside-Down Gas Market Europe's New Normal?
articles.author: Javier Blas

The situation raises concerns about heating costs for families and potential inflation. Various players, including traders and regulators, are involved in deciding how to handle the storage issue.

articles.title: Don’t Fall for the Hype About China’s Critical Minerals Controls
articles.author: Javier Blas
articles.summary: The U.S. produces enough molybdenum to meet its needs, despite concerns over China's export controls on critical minerals. The hype around potential shortages is exaggerated, and the U.S. will be fine. Many companies have rebranded to highlight these minerals as "critical" to boost their appeal and share prices.

articles.title: What’s wrong with democracy in Europe?

Policymakers are adjusting targets and regulations, but there are concerns about entering winter with potentially low gas stockpiles.

articles.title: Rare Earths in Ukraine? No, Only Scorched Earth.

articles.title: The root of all evil?
articles.author: David Weakliem (noreply@blogger.com)
articles.summary: Clarence Thomas recently gave a speech at the University of Texas--Austin that's attracted a lot of attention.  Thomas said that a new philosophy of "progressivism," which rejected the principles of the Declaration of Independence, emerged in the early 20th century and "has made many inroads into our system of government and our way of life."    In his account, progressives don't believe in "universal, unalienable natural rights"; they believe "that our rights and our dignities come . . . from government."  Of course, it's unusual for a Supreme Court justice to make such an explicitly political statement.  But another noteworthy point is that his characterization of progressivism is not just wrong, but the direct opposite of the truth.  The early 20th century progressives saw the American Revolution as having two sides:  the spirit of democracy and the interests of the propertied classes (including slaveowners).  They held that the Declaration of Independence represented the first and the Constitution (mostly) represented the second.  The implication was that when considering proposed reforms, the focus should be on whether they helped to secure the rights of the people rather than on whether they followed tradition or were explicitly authorized by the Constitution.  That is, progressives didn't reject the idea of individual rights existing prior to government, they put them at the center of their philosophy.Thomas is correct in saying that the progressives saw rights as "historically conditioned," but that didn't mean that they thought rights were determined by governments.  Rather, it meant that new social conditions could give rise to new rights, which governments should then recognize:  for example, when education becomes necessary in order to get ahead, then people have a right to education, and the government has an obligation to see that people get an education.  One of his examples of the evils of progressivism is a 1927 Supreme Court decision upholding a...
